join_order.notes: Build dense adsh->(sic,cik) filter first; retain only pre triples whose adsh survives SIC predicate.

join_order.build: pre(filtered: stmt='EQ' AND adsh in filtered sub)
join_order.probe: num rows from num_adsh_fk_hash postings for filtered adsh
join_order.notes: Compact triple hash set on (adsh,tag,version) after SIC pruning; avoids cache-exceeding num triple posting index.

join_order.build: tag(filtered: abstract=0 and key in needed pairs)
join_order.notes: Materialize (tag,version)->tlabel only for keys observed in retained pre triples.

data_structures.dim_filter: flat_array(adsh->sub_rowid) + bitset(filtered_adsh) + compact_hash_map((tag,version)->tlabel for needed pairs)
data_structures.fact_join: compact_hash_set((adsh,tag,version) from pre stmt='EQ' intersect filtered_adsh)

optimization_notes: Root cause is wasted work and startup-heavy physical choices: the prior plan deserializes and probes cache-exceeding triple index structures before the selective SIC predicate is applied, so data_loading and build_joins dominate. Strategy Category C (work elimination) with plan restructuring applies sub SIC filtering first, intersects pre(stmt='EQ') with those adsh values to build a much smaller triple semi-join set, then probes num through num_adsh_fk_hash only for surviving filings; tag lookup is materialized only for needed (tag,version) keys. This removes most unnecessary startup parsing and reduces probe work to rows proportional to filtered adsh output.
